clinical_history: ECG from an elderly man with severe aortic stenosis shows which ONE of the following mechanisms?
diagnosis: The ECG shows an irregular, narrow (QRS) complex tachycardia at about 100/min on average. The instantaneous rate range is roughly between 80 and 140/min. There are bursts of multiple discrete P waves of varying morphology (at least 3 or more, as in leads II, III and aVF), consistent with intermittent multifocal atrial tachycardia/rhythm (MAT/MAR). High frequency baseline noise adds to potential confusion with atrial fibrillation. The QRS axis is leftward (-35 degrees) and there is at least borderline voltage criterion for left ventricular hypertrophy (R in aVL of about 12 mm.) Non-specific ST-T changes are present. The broad negative P wave in lead V1 is consistent with left atrial abnormality. ~~
